name: Hearthglen
region: Northern Lordaeron, fortified town and military staging ground on the southern line of the plague response.

sensoryProfile.sight: Stone walls, square towers, the chapel roof over the inner line, a gate black-scored by holy fire, a patched western tower, triage blankets over mud, stacked wood, burn pits, and pyres smoking outside the walls.
sensoryProfile.sound: Bells starting again before the last peal dies, priests shouting over stretchers, quartermasters and officers calling through the camp, and the hard interruption of bodies rising where the living hoped they were handling corpses.
sensoryProfile.smell: Smoke, mud, horse sweat, lamp fat, and the thick mixed smell of priests and soldiers handling one body at a time.
sensoryProfile.touch: Heat off holy fire, churned mud under boots, stiff fresh reins at the horse line, and the raw physical press of a town being forced to stay alive minute by minute.
sensoryProfile.atmosphere: Chapter 15 makes Hearthglen fully real as a fortified town surviving by brutal rhythm rather than safety. It has held, but only in the narrowest and ugliest sense.

establishedFacts.fact: Hearthglen supports a large disciplined field force under Arthas with enough infrastructure to mobilize rapidly.
The camp is already treating sickness the Light does not easily answer, which means the plague has reached the force before the briefing finishes.
Its defenses and rhythms are configured for visible war even while the real threat is moving through grain and stores.
By chapter 13 Hearthglen is already overwhelmed, so it can no longer function as a stable fallback point or delayed briefing site.
Hearthglen held only provisionally and nearly failed again after dark after the first crisis wave.
Bodies at Hearthglen keep answering after short lulls, forcing the field into immediate burning before families are ready.
Uther remains on the field at Hearthglen even after Arthas drives the main body onward toward Stratholme.
Hearthglen is a real fortified town with walls, towers, chapel, smithy, stables, and enough infrastructure to keep fighting while also serving as triage and command ground.
The town survives by immediate corpse management: if a body lies unburned too long, someone has to stay with it because it may rise before the fire.
Holy fire has become ordinary field equipment inside Hearthglen rather than exceptional ritual.
Arthas’s command pavilion sits in the lee of the south wall, making the town both battlefield and war-room at once.

eventsHere.event: Sylvanas forces access through the checkpoint and briefs Arthas on the plague as a grain-road war.
Arthas turns the briefing into immediate granary controls and mounted mobilization toward Hearthglen’s southern line.
Uther privately names Arthas’s flaw outside the pavilion while the column forms around them.
A south-track relay reports that Hearthglen has been overwhelmed and Arthas is already moving on Stratholme, collapsing the trio's available time to almost nothing.
Relay traffic reports that Hearthglen nearly fails again after dark, with the wounded burned early because the dead keep standing back up.
Arthas does not remain to consolidate Hearthglen; he takes the main body south-east toward Stratholme while Uther stays tied to the still-failing field.
The road party enters Hearthglen through an active triage gate and watches a corpse rise before it reaches the pyre, proving the town is holding only by repeated hard interruption.
Uther gives the field read from the middle of triage and then leads Sylvanas and Jaina to Arthas’s command tent.
Arthas receives the full plague picture in Hearthglen and launches both the main march on Stratholme and Jaina’s smaller head-start lane from the same camp.
